channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Quote of the day by SpaceX CEO Elon Musk: "If you go back say, 300 years, the things we take for granted today, you'd be burned at stake for" — echoes of science fiction
item.description: Elon Musk reflects on advances we've made in the 21st century and how, in times gone past, they would be considered "indistinguishable from magic"

item.title: 'A total gamechanger for big bass in a small space': I heard a groundbreaking new subwoofer driver that fits in a tiny box 6x smaller than a regular sub, but can punch you in the gut like it's a home theater beast with its 'powerful but tight' sound
item.description: GSS Audio's innovative transducer can put the power of a 10-inch sub in a box the size of a microwave dinner — and it's designed to work in soundbars or wall-mounted speakers too.

item.title: Nearly a million passports and photo IDs were left unprotected on the public internet
item.description: Typing a few letters and numbers into my web browser, I find myself gaping at the identity documents of complete strangers. The passport of a young woman from Germany. The passport of a man from Spain with glasses resting on his head. The front and back of another man's driver's license, a stereotypically goofy expression […]

item.title: Researchers say they trained a foundation model from scratch for about $1,500
item.description: Training a foundation LLM from scratch costs millions and requires internet-scale data — which is why most enterprises don't bother. Sapient thinks it has a cheaper path.To overcome this brute-force scaling dogma, researchers at Sapient developed HRM-Text, which replaces standard Transformers with a highly sample-efficient Hierarchical Recurrent Model (HRM), an architecture they first introduced last year.HRM decouples computation into slow-evolving strategic and fast-evolving execution layers. Instead of brute-force autoregressive prediction on raw text, HRM-Text trains exclusively on instruc...

item.title: Cybercriminals claim breach of Oracle PeopleSoft servers at 100-plus organizations
item.description: The ShinyHunters hacking gang claims to have compromised the Oracle PeopleSoft servers of more than 100 organizations, including many universities.

item.title: Xbox warns of a ‘reset’ as it prepares for layoffs
item.description: Microsoft's Xbox division will be hit with significant layoffs next month, according to people familiar with Microsoft's plans. The company has been preparing for the layoffs internally for weeks, with Xbox CEO Asha Sharma hinting about "making hard choices" last month. Sources suggest the cuts could even involve a studio closure, or changes to the […]...

item.title: Experts warn hackers are hiding malware inside Google's own ad systems — here's what we know
item.description: Hackers routed a multi-stage malware campaign through Google's ad infrastructure, using dynamic branding and in-memory execution to evade detection entirely.

item.title: I’ve witnessed BYD fully charge its Denza Z9 GT in under 10 minutes, and it’s going to change how people view EVs
item.description: Five-minute EV ‘Flash charging’ is coming to the UK and Europe this year, promising ultra-rapid top-ups at a surprisingly low price

item.title: CISA Tells US Agencies to Fix Security Bugs in as Little as 3 Days Thanks to AI Threats
item.description: “Defenders cannot afford to take weeks to patch,” one Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency official warned on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Risks Key Surveillance Authority Over ‘Unqualified’ Spy-Chief Pick
item.description: US lawmakers are alarmed that Bill Pulte, a housing official with no intelligence experience, is poised to take charge of one of the government's most powerful surveillance tools.

item.title: Kai Cenat relaunches Streamer University — heres how to apply
item.description: Viral streamer Kai Cenat ushers in return to social media with second annual Streamer University.

item.title: Apple, Google add support for Thread 1.4
item.description: Apple and Google are updating their smart home streaming devices to Thread 1.4. As first spotted by Matter Alpha and 9to5 Google, the latest spec has arrived on compatible Apple TVs in the tvOS 27 developer beta and the Google TV Streamer through a software update. This lays the groundwork for these devices, which serve […]

item.title: Nashville Zoo leads fight against mega AI data center — over 375,000 sign petition against 69,000 square foot building set to overlook animal habitats
item.description: Nashville Zoo collects 375k signatures against a proposed data center, citing potential animal welfare and conservation risks.

item.title: Everyone wants a piece of Tesla’s battery business
item.description: Electricity demand from AI data centers is pushing everyone — including automakers like GM and Ford — into the energy storage business.

item.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
item.description: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

item.title: Kalshi adds required employment verification for some prediction market bets
item.description: The CFTC is considering its first regulation for prediction markets, as arrests over "insider trading" on everything from military operations to Google Search data continue to stack up. As CoinDesk reports, a notice of proposed rulemaking says "the proposal would establish a structured framework for evaluating whether such contracts involve an activity enumerated in Section […]...

item.title: Valve will stop producing physical Steam gift cards because of scammers
item.description: The never-ending problem of scammers has made Valve decide to stop producing Steam gift cards.
